text: The spell of extreme heat this month has seen the UK record for June broken.
zh: 本月这波极端高温已经打破了英国六月的气温纪录。

text: The new top temperature for this month was just over 36 degrees, recorded in Gosport in Hampshire.
zh: 本月最高气温刚超过36度，出现在汉普郡的戈斯波特。

text: The Met Office has extended the current red extreme heat warning, meaning a risk to life, until the end of tomorrow.
zh: 英国气象局已将当前的极端高温红色预警延长至明天午夜前，这意味着存在生命危险。

text: It now covers more parts of southern England across Dorset, Hampshire and Sussex.
zh: 该预警现已覆盖英格兰南部更多地区，包括多塞特郡、汉普郡和苏塞克斯郡。

text: It's also issued another amber warning for heat for Friday, covering eastern and central England.
zh: 气象局还针对周五发布了另一项高温橙色预警，覆盖英格兰东部和中部地区。

text: People stranded in heavy traffic for hours following a serious crash between a van and lorry on the M25
zh: 在M25公路上，一辆货车与一辆卡车发生严重相撞，导致大量车辆滞留数小时，

text: have been treated for heat -related illnesses.
zh: 有人因中暑相关疾病接受了治疗。

text: Rail passengers were told not to travel unless it was completely essential.
zh: 铁路乘客被告知，除非绝对必要，否则不要出行。

text: More than 1 ,000 schools in England and Wales were shut or closed early because of today's soaring temperatures and there's more on the way.
zh: 英格兰和威尔士有超过1000所学校因今日气温飙升而停课或提前放学，而更多高温还在后头。

text: Helena Wilkinson reports.
zh: 请看海伦娜·威尔金森的报道。

text: Sunrise this morning and many parts of the UK were braced for a day of soaring temperatures.
zh: 今天清晨，英国许多地区都在为又一天的高温做好准备。

text: The impact today across many parts of our lives has been severe.
zh: 今天，高温对我们生活的方方面面都造成了严重影响。

text: This morning, commuters struggled with the already uncomfortable heat under the blazing sun.
zh: 今天上午，在炙热阳光下，通勤者已经感受到令人难受的高温。

text: Rail passengers faced cancellations and delays and were warned not to travel to, from or within the red weather zone unless absolutely necessary.
zh: 铁路乘客面临取消和延误，并被警告除非绝对必要，否则不要前往、离开或穿越红色预警区域。

text: Tonight, South East Coast Ambulance Service are treating patients stranded on the M25
zh: 今晚，东南海岸救护服务部门正在为滞留在M25公路上的患者提供治疗，

text: for heat -related illnesses after an earlier serious crash.
zh: 这些患者是在早些时候的一起严重车祸后，因中暑相关疾病而就医的。

text: Many have been stuck in long queues for hours.
zh: 许多人已经在长长的车龙中被困数小时。

text: And Queen Alexandra Hospital in Portsmouth has declared a critical incident after a cooling system supporting critical infrastructure failed during the heatwave.
zh: 朴茨茅斯的亚历山德拉女王医院，在支持关键基础设施的制冷系统在热浪中失灵后，宣布进入重大事件状态。

text: Many schools have been without pupils today.
zh: 今天许多学校都没有学生上课。

text: In England and Wales more than a thousand were shut or closed early because of the extreme heat.
zh: 在英格兰和威尔士，因极端高温，有超过一千所学校停课或提前放学。

text: This school in Cardiff was closed at lunchtime and will be shut tomorrow.
zh: 加的夫的这所学校中午就已停课，明天也将停课一整天。

text: The classrooms on one side of the building are
zh: 学校一侧的教室

text: quite really bad with with temperature at the moment so we decided that it was the safest thing to do because it's not comfortable for the pupils and because of that they don't really learn that much anyway so we decided for everybody's health and well -being to just
zh: 目前的温度实在太高了，所以我们决定这样做是最安全的，因为对学生来说太不舒服，而且在这种情况下他们也学不进去什么，所以我们决定为了大家的健康和福祉，

text: close this afternoon and close all day tomorrow
zh: 今天下午停课，明天全天停课。

text: six -year -old Harris's school in West London will also be closed tomorrow his mother understands why but is frustrated
zh: 六岁的哈里斯在伦敦西区的学校明天也将停课，他的母亲表示理解，但也感到无奈。

text: it is really tough I was relatively lucky this This week I didn't have too many fixed meetings, but I need to be in the office most days a week.
zh: 这真的很艰难，这周我算是比较幸运的，没有太多固定的会议，但我每周大部分时间还是得去办公室。

text: The biggest risk here is that this isn't a one -off, I don't think.
zh: 我认为最大的风险在于，这不会是一次性的事件。

text: It's going to keep occurring, and so we do need some sort of a plan and a strategy around it,
zh: 这种情况会不断出现，所以我们确实需要某种应对计划和策略，

text: and probably some funding to support the schools, because they just can't cope right now.
zh: 也许还需要一些资金来支持学校，因为它们目前实在难以应付。

text: Some people have been able to work from home today, but for others, working outside has been unavoidable.
zh: 今天有些人得以居家办公，但对另一些人来说，户外工作是无法避免的。

text: Horrible. It's not very nice, there's no shade, you've got no let -up.
zh: 太糟糕了，一点也不舒服，没有遮阳的地方，一刻都喘不过气来。

text: Yeah, you can see it out here, it's baking.
zh: 是啊，你也能看到，这里简直像在烤炉里。

text: As the high temperatures continue, vulnerable people in particular have been urged to take care.
zh: 随着高温持续，尤其是脆弱人群，被呼吁要格外小心。

text: It's just about making sure that you are staying as cool as you can.
zh: 关键在于尽量让自己保持凉爽。

text: If it's cooler outside, keep your windows open, keep your curtains closed during the day if it's sun facing.
zh: 如果外面比较凉快，就把窗户打开；如果阳光直射，白天要拉上窗帘。

text: If you've got a fan at home, stick it on, stick an ice bowl in front of it, lots of cool baths.
zh: 如果家里有风扇，就打开它，在前面放一盆冰，多泡几次凉水澡。

text: If you've got young children, get the paddling pool out in the shade and let them play.
zh: 如果家里有小孩，可以把戏水池搬到阴凉处，让孩子玩耍。

text: just doing fun things, but things that you can to keep yourself cool.
zh: 做一些有趣但又能帮助你保持凉爽的事情。

text: Even the King was feeling the heat today as he attended a London Climate Week reception at St James's Palace.
zh: 就连国王今天在圣詹姆士宫出席伦敦气候周招待会时，也感受到了这股热浪。

text: Luckily, a royal aide was close by with a fan to keep him cool.
zh: 幸好一位王室侍从及时在旁边为他扇风降温。

text: The red warning for extreme heat in parts of England and Wales is due to continue until just before midnight on Thursday.
zh: 英格兰和威尔士部分地区的极端高温红色预警将持续到周四午夜前。

text: with little respite ahead people have been urged to stay safe in the extreme conditions Helena Wilkinson BBC News
zh: 由于短期内几乎没有缓解迹象，人们被呼吁在极端天气下注意安全。以上是海伦娜·威尔金森为BBC新闻带来的报道。

text: while Europe has also struggled to cope with a record -breaking heat wave so far France Spain and Italy have been hardest hit
zh: 与此同时，欧洲也在努力应对一场破纪录的热浪，目前法国、西班牙和意大利受灾最为严重。

text: today was the hottest June day on record for Spain in one region Cantabria the mercury hit nearly 44 Celsius the highest temperature on record for any month of the year.
zh: 今天是西班牙有记录以来六月最热的一天，其中坎塔布里亚地区气温达到近44摄氏度，创下该地区全年任何月份的最高纪录。

text: France has had its hottest day since records began in 1947.
zh: 法国也迎来了自1947年有记录以来最热的一天。

text: The temperature reached over 40 Celsius in Paris.
zh: 巴黎气温一度超过40摄氏度。

text: Several landmarks were closed or shut early and thousands of schools were closed across the country for at least part of the day.
zh: 多个地标建筑被关闭或提前关闭，全国也有数千所学校至少停课了部分时间。

text: Red alerts were in place for 16 cities in Italy, including Rome, Milan, Florence and Turin.
zh: 意大利有16个城市发布了红色预警，其中包括罗马、米兰、佛罗伦萨和都灵。

text: At St Peter's Square in the Vatican, hundreds of people waited in the intense heat to see the Pope using whatever they could, shawls, fans and umbrellas to protect themselves from the heat.
zh: 在梵蒂冈的圣彼得广场，数百人在酷热中等待面见教皇，他们用披肩、扇子和雨伞等各种方式为自己遮阳降温。

text: So why are we experiencing such unusually hot weather?
zh: 那么，为什么我们会经历如此异常的高温天气呢？

text: Well, our climate editor Justin Rowlatt is in Gosport, which recorded the hottest temperature in the UK today.
zh: 我们的气候编辑贾斯汀·罗拉特现在正在戈斯波特，那里录得今天英国的最高气温。

text: Justin.
zh: 贾斯汀。

text: It certainly did. It was a brutal 36 .1 degrees Celsius, as you say.
zh: 没错，正如你所说，那是极为酷热的36.1摄氏度。

text: That's the provisional figure, a full half degree hotter than the previous June record.
zh: 这是初步数据，比此前的六月纪录整整高出0.5度。

text: And people often say, do these tumbling temperature records really matter?
zh: 人们常常会问，这些不断被打破的气温纪录真的重要吗？

text: We've always had hot days. We've always had heat waves.
zh: 我们一直都有炎热的日子，也一直都有热浪。

text: And they are right up to a point.
zh: 从某种程度上说，这话没错。

text: Yes, we've always had heat waves.
zh: 是的，我们一直都有热浪。

text: We know why they happen. High pressure, clear skies, light winds, lots of sunshine.
zh: 我们也知道它们为什么会发生：高气压、晴朗的天空、微风，还有充足的阳光。

text: Heat really builds. But take a look at this.
zh: 热量因此不断累积。但请看这个。

text: chart shows the rise in global temperatures since the pre -industrial era.
zh: 这张图表显示了自工业化前时代以来全球气温的上升情况。

text: You've probably seen it before.
zh: 你可能之前也见过这张图。

text: And look at how the temperatures take off in the
zh: 你看气温在

text: 1970s. And so we now get the same weather patterns, but in that warmer world.
zh: 1970年代开始急剧上升。所以现在我们经历的仍是同样的天气模式，只不过是在一个更炎热的世界里。

text: And it means the heat waves are hotter.
zh: 这意味着热浪变得更热了，

text: They last for longer and they're more frequent.
zh: 持续时间更长，出现的频率也更高。

text: I mean, think about it. In a world without climate change, you wouldn't expect temperature records to be broken.
zh: 想想看，如果没有气候变化，我们本不该看到气温纪录被不断打破。

text: But every single one of the last four decades has been hotter than the last.
zh: 但过去四十年里，每一个十年都比前一个十年更热。

text: So I've said it before, Rita, and I'm sure I'll say it again.
zh: 所以我以前说过，丽塔，我相信我以后还会再说：

text: Yes, this is weather, but this is weather in a warming world.
zh: 是的，这是天气，但这是发生在一个正在变暖的世界里的天气。
